title: Overload operator<< for a Season Enum
prompt_md:
## Goal
Make an unscoped `Season` enum print as a human-readable name by overloading `operator<<`, so that writing `std::cout << season` produces the season name directly.

Season codes: `0` = Spring, `1` = Summer, `2` = Autumn, `3` = Winter.

## Input
- A single integer (0-3).

## Output
One line:
```
The season is <SeasonName>
```
where `<SeasonName>` is `Spring`, `Summer`, `Autumn`, or `Winter`.

## Constraints
- Define an unscoped `enum Season`.
- Overload `operator<<` (a free function returning `std::ostream&`) so the season streams as its name. The print site in `main` must stream the `Season` value directly (e.g. `std::cout << s`), not call a name-lookup helper itself.
- Input is always 0-3.

title: Overload Both I/O Operators for a Direction Enum
prompt_md:
## Goal
Make an unscoped `Direction` enum work seamlessly with streams by overloading both `operator>>` (to read a direction) and `operator<<` (to print one).

Direction codes: `0` = North, `1` = East, `2` = South, `3` = West. Any other code is treated as invalid and prints as `Invalid`.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 5).
- Next `N` lines: each contains a single integer direction code (may be out of range).

## Output
Exactly `N` lines, each:
```
Heading: <DirectionName>
```
where `<DirectionName>` is `North`, `East`, `South`, `West`, or `Invalid`.

## Constraints
- Define an unscoped `enum Direction`.
- Overload `operator>>` (free function, `std::istream&` and `Direction&`) to read an integer and store the matching direction, recording out-of-range input as an invalid value.
- Overload `operator<<` (free function, returning `std::ostream&`) to print the direction's name.
- In `main`, read each direction with `>>` and print it with `<<`; the loop body must not do the int-to-name mapping itself.

test_cases.label: Sample -- three valid directions
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Heading: North
Heading: South
Heading: East

test_cases.label: Hidden -- includes invalid direction code
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Heading: West
Heading: East
Heading: Invalid
Heading: North
